title: Why Fewer Babies Are Being Born in the U.S.
summary: In the United States, fewer babies are being born now than before. This is called a low birth rate. Since 2007, the number of babies born has dropped by 23%. This means there are not enough new children to replace people who grow old or pass away. If this continues, by 2055, the U.S. might have 8 million fewer people. But there is some good news: fewer teenagers are having babies, which experts say is positive. The article talks about why this is happening and what it could mean for the future, like having fewer workers or changes in schools. It is based on data from the CDC and includes an interview with a reporter from NPR.

background_read: The U.S. birth rate is how many babies are born each year. When it is low, it can affect things like how many people work or go to school. This article explains why fewer babies are being born and what might happen in the future, using simple examples to help kids understand.
Article_Structure: WHO: The CDC and NPR reporter Brian Mann
WHAT: The U.S. birth rate is dropping, meaning fewer babies are born
WHY: To explain why this is happening and what it means for the future

perspectives.description: They say fewer teen pregnancies are good for society.
